sentences.sentence: The Lumière Brothers opened their Cinematographe at 14 Boulevard des Capucines in Paris, to 100 paying customers over 100 years ago, on December 8, 1895.
sentences.translation: 100多年前，也就是1895年12月8日，卢米埃尔兄弟在巴黎卡普辛大道14号为100名付费观众开设了他们的电影放映机。

structure.text: at 14 Boulevard des Capucines in Paris

difficult_points: to 100 paying customers 表示放映对象，而不是 opened 的地点。
over 100 years ago 和 on December 8, 1895 都是时间状语，补充说明事件发生时间。

sentences.sentence: Before the eyes of the stunned, thrilled audience, photographs came to life and moved across a flat screen.
sentences.translation: 在惊愕而兴奋的观众眼前，照片仿佛活了起来，在平面屏幕上移动。

structure.text: Before the eyes of the stunned, thrilled audience
came to life and moved across a flat screen

difficult_points: came to life 是固定表达，意为‘变得生动起来’或‘仿佛活了起来’。
stunned, thrilled 是并列形容词，分别说明观众震惊和兴奋的状态。

sentences.sentence: So ordinary and routine has this become to us that it takes a determined leap of the imagination to grasp the impact of those first moving images.
sentences.translation: 这对我们来说已经变得如此普通和习以为常，以至于要理解最初那些动态影像的冲击力，必须进行一次有意识的想象飞跃。

structure.text: that it takes a determined leap of the imagination to grasp the impact of those first moving images
to grasp the impact of those first moving images

difficult_points: So...that... 构成结果状语从句，主句使用倒装：So ordinary and routine has this become。
it takes...to... 中 it 是形式主语，真正主语是后面的不定式 to grasp...。

sentences.sentence: But it is worth trying, for to understand the initial shock of those images is to understand the extraordinary power and magic of cinema, the unique, hypnotic quality that has made film the most dynamic, effective art form of the 20th century.
sentences.translation: 但这值得一试，因为理解那些影像最初带来的震撼，就是理解电影非凡的力量和魔力——正是这种独特而令人着迷的品质，使电影成为20世纪最富有活力、最具影响力的艺术形式。

structure.text: to understand the initial shock of those images
is to understand the extraordinary power and magic of cinema
that has made film the most dynamic, effective art form of the 20th century

difficult_points: for 引导解释原因；to understand... is to understand... 是两个不定式短语构成的对等结构。
that has made... 修饰 the unique, hypnotic quality，make 后接宾语和宾语补足语。

sentences.sentence: One of the Lumière Brothers' earliest films was a 30-second piece which showed a section of a railway platform flooded with sunshine.
sentences.translation: 卢米埃尔兄弟最早的电影之一是一部30秒的短片，展示了阳光洒满的一段火车站站台。

structure.text: One of the Lumière Brothers' earliest films
which showed a section of a railway platform flooded with sunshine

difficult_points: which 引导非限制性定语从句，修饰 a 30-second piece。
flooded with sunshine 是过去分词短语，后置修饰 a railway platform，表示‘被阳光洒满的’。

sentences.sentence: A train appears and heads straight for the camera.
sentences.translation: 一列火车出现了，直冲镜头而来。

structure.text: appears and heads straight for the camera

difficult_points: appears 和 heads 是并列谓语，描述连续发生的两个动作。
head straight for 表示‘径直朝……前进’，不是 head 的身体部位含义。

sentences.sentence: And that is all that happens.
sentences.translation: 事情就只有这些。
difficult_points: all that happens 是定语从句，that 指代发生的全部事情。
And 放在句首承接上文，强调情节极其简单。

sentences.sentence: Yet the Russian director Andrei Tarkovsky, one of the greatest of all film artists, described the film as a 'work of genius'.
sentences.translation: 然而，俄罗斯导演安德烈·塔可夫斯基——最伟大的电影艺术家之一——把这部电影称为‘天才之作’。

structure.text: one of the greatest of all film artists
described the film as a 'work of genius'

difficult_points: one of the greatest of all film artists 是同位语，补充说明 Tarkovsky 的身份。
describe A as B 表示‘把A描述为B’。

sentences.sentence: 'As the train approached,' wrote Tarkovsky, 'panic started in the theatre: people jumped and ran away.
sentences.translation: 塔可夫斯基写道：‘火车驶近时，剧院里开始恐慌：人们跳起来逃跑。’
difficult_points: As 引导时间状语从句，表示‘当……时’。
引述语中 people jumped and ran away 与前面的 panic started 并列说明恐慌的表现。

sentences.sentence: That was the moment when cinema was born.
sentences.translation: 那一刻，电影诞生了。

structure.text: the moment when cinema was born

difficult_points: when cinema was born 是定语从句，修饰 the moment。
was born 是被动语态，字面是‘被诞生’，这里表示电影诞生。

sentences.sentence: The frightened audience could not accept that they were watching a mere picture.
sentences.translation: 受惊的观众无法接受自己看到的只是一幅画面。

structure.text: that they were watching a mere picture

difficult_points: that 引导 accept 的宾语从句，they 指代 the audience。
were watching 使用过去进行时，表示当时正在观看的动作。

sentences.sentence: Pictures were still, only reality moved; this must, therefore, be reality.
sentences.translation: 画面本来是静止的，只有现实会移动；因此，这一定就是现实。
difficult_points: 分号连接两个相关判断，only 强调‘只有现实’会移动。
must be 表示根据当时认知作出的必然推断。

sentences.sentence: In their confusion, they feared that a real train was about to crush them.'
sentences.translation: 在困惑之中，他们害怕一列真正的火车即将把自己撞倒。

structure.text: that a real train was about to crush them

difficult_points: that 引导 feared 的宾语从句，them 指代观众。
was about to 表示即将发生的动作，说明观众以为危险迫在眉睫。

sentences.sentence: Early cinema audiences often experienced the same confusion.
sentences.translation: 早期电影观众常常经历同样的困惑。
difficult_points: Early cinema 是名词作定语，意为‘早期电影’。
the same confusion 指代上文观众把影像误认为现实的困惑。

sentences.sentence: In time, the idea of film became familiar, the magic was accepted—but it never stopped being magic.
sentences.translation: 随着时间推移，电影的概念变得熟悉，人们接受了它的魔力——但它从未停止具有魔力。

structure.text: the idea of film became familiar

difficult_points: the magic was accepted 使用被动语态，主语是 magic。
stop being magic 中 being 是动名词，stop 后接动名词表示停止做某事；破折号引出转折。

sentences.sentence: Film has never lost its unique power to embrace its audiences and transport them to a different world.
sentences.translation: 电影从未失去拥抱观众、将他们带入另一个世界的独特力量。

structure.text: to embrace its audiences and transport them to a different world

difficult_points: has never lost 是现在完成时，表示这种力量从过去一直延续到现在。
to embrace... and transport... 是并列不定式，说明 power 的具体作用。

sentences.sentence: For Tarkovsky, the key to that magic was the way in which cinema created a dynamic image of the real flow of events.
sentences.translation: 对塔可夫斯基而言，这种魔力的关键在于电影呈现真实事件流动过程的方式。

structure.text: the way in which cinema created a dynamic image of the real flow of events
in which cinema created a dynamic image of the real flow of events

difficult_points: the way in which 引导定语从句，说明‘方式’的具体内容。
the real flow of events 中 of events 修饰 flow，表示事件的真实流动。

sentences.sentence: A still picture could only imply the existence of time, while time in a novel passed at the whim of the reader.
sentences.translation: 静止的画面只能暗示时间的存在，而小说中的时间则随着读者的意愿流逝。

structure.text: while time in a novel passed at the whim of the reader

difficult_points: while 在此表示对比，而不是‘当……时’。
at the whim of the reader 表示时间推进取决于读者，whim 指一时兴致。

sentences.sentence: But in cinema, the real, objective flow of time was captured.
sentences.translation: 但在电影中，真实而客观的时间流动被捕捉了下来。

structure.text: the real, objective flow of time

difficult_points: was captured 是一般过去时被动语态，强调时间流动被电影记录下来。
real 和 objective 是并列形容词，共同修饰 flow of time。

sentences.sentence: One effect of this realism was to educate the world about itself.
sentences.translation: 这种现实主义的一个作用，是让世界了解自身。

structure.text: to educate the world about itself

difficult_points: One effect of... 是主语，of this realism 表示所属关系。
itself 指代 the world，about itself 表示‘了解自身’。

sentences.sentence: For cinema makes the world smaller.
sentences.translation: 因为电影让世界变小了。
difficult_points: 句首 For 表示原因，解释上一句电影如何教育世界。
make 后接宾语 the world 和宾语补足语 smaller，构成‘使……变得……’。

sentences.sentence: Long before people travelled to America or anywhere else, they knew what other places looked like; they knew how other people worked and lived.
sentences.translation: 早在人们去美国或其他地方旅行之前，他们就知道其他地方是什么样子；他们也知道其他人如何工作和生活。

structure.text: Long before people travelled to America or anywhere else
they knew what other places looked like
they knew how other people worked and lived
how other people worked and lived

difficult_points: Long before 引导时间状语从句，表示‘早在……之前’。
what other places looked like 和 how other people worked and lived 都是 know 的宾语从句。

sentences.sentence: Overwhelmingly, the lives recorded—at least in film fiction—have been American.
sentences.translation: 总体而言，被记录下来的生活——至少在电影虚构作品中——一直是美国人的生活。
difficult_points: recorded 是过去分词后置修饰 the lives，意为‘被记录的生活’。
at least in film fiction 是插入语，对范围作限制说明。

sentences.sentence: From the earliest days of the industry, Hollywood has dominated the world film market.
sentences.translation: 从电影业最早的时期起，好莱坞就一直主导着世界电影市场。

structure.text: From the earliest days of the industry

difficult_points: has dominated 是现在完成时，表示从过去开始并持续至今。
From the earliest days of the industry 表示时间起点。

sentences.sentence: American imagery—the cars, the cities, the cowboys—became the primary imagery of film.
sentences.translation: 美国意象——汽车、城市和牛仔——成为电影中的主要意象。

structure.text: the cars, the cities, the cowboys

difficult_points: 两个破折号之间是同位语，具体列举 American imagery 的内容。
became 后接主语补足语，说明 American imagery 最终变成什么。

sentences.sentence: Film carried American life and values around the globe.
sentences.translation: 电影把美国的生活方式和价值观传播到全球。
difficult_points: carried 在此是比喻用法，表示传播而非单纯搬运。
American life and values 是并列宾语。

sentences.sentence: And, thanks to film, future generations will know the 20th century more intimately than any other period.
sentences.translation: 而且，多亏了电影，未来世代会比了解其他任何时期都更深入地了解20世纪。

structure.text: the 20th century more intimately than any other period

difficult_points: thanks to film 是介词短语，表示原因‘多亏电影’。
more intimately than any other period 是比较结构，省略了与 know 相关的部分。

sentences.sentence: We can only imagine what life was like in the 14th century or in classical Greece.
sentences.translation: 我们只能想象14世纪或古希腊的生活是什么样的。

structure.text: what life was like in the 14th century or in classical Greece

difficult_points: what life was like 是 imagine 的宾语从句，what 在从句中作表语。
can only 表示能力受到限制，意为‘只能’。

sentences.sentence: But the life of the modern world has been recorded on film in massive, encyclopaedic detail.
sentences.translation: 但现代世界的生活已经以海量、百科全书般详尽的细节被记录在电影中。

structure.text: the life of the modern world

difficult_points: has been recorded 是现在完成时被动语态，强调记录已完成且影响现在。
massive 和 encyclopaedic 是并列形容词，修饰 detail。

sentences.sentence: We shall be known better than any preceding generations.
sentences.translation: 我们将比此前的任何一代人都更为人所知。
difficult_points: shall be known 是将来时被动语态，表示未来人们对我们的了解程度。
better than any preceding generations 是比较结构，省略了‘被了解’。

sentences.sentence: The 'star' was another natural consequence of cinema.
sentences.translation: ‘明星’是电影带来的另一个自然结果。
difficult_points: star 用引号标出概念，指电影明星现象。
consequence of cinema 表示电影产生的结果。

sentences.sentence: The cinema star was effectively born in 1910.
sentences.translation: 电影明星实际上诞生于1910年。
difficult_points: was born 是被动结构，表示‘诞生’；effectively 表示实际上。
in 1910 是时间状语。

sentences.sentence: Film personalities have such an immediate presence that, inevitably, they become super-real.
sentences.translation: 电影人物具有如此直接的存在感，以至于他们不可避免地变得超现实。
difficult_points: such...that... 构成结果状语从句，说明存在感带来的结果。
inevitably 是插入的连接副词，表示‘不可避免地’。

sentences.sentence: Because we watch them so closely and because everybody in the world seems to know who they are, they appear more real to us than we do ourselves.
sentences.translation: 因为我们如此近距离地观看他们，而且世界上的每个人似乎都知道他们是谁，所以他们在我们眼中比我们自己显得更真实。

structure.text: Because we watch them so closely
because everybody in the world seems to know who they are
they appear more real to us than we do ourselves

difficult_points: 两个 Because 从句并列说明原因，they 指代 film personalities。
than we do ourselves 是比较结构，do 代替 appear more real，避免重复。

sentences.sentence: The star as magnified human self is one of cinema's most strange and enduring legacies.
sentences.translation: 作为被放大的人类自我的明星，是电影最奇特、最持久的遗产之一。

structure.text: The star as magnified human self
one of cinema's most strange and enduring legacies

difficult_points: as magnified human self 表示‘作为被放大的人类自我’，说明 star 的角色。
one of 后接复数名词 legacies，表示‘……之一’。

sentences.sentence: Cinema has also given a new lease of life to the idea of the story.
sentences.translation: 电影还让故事这一概念重新焕发了生机。

structure.text: to the idea of the story

difficult_points: give a new lease of life to 是固定表达，意为‘赋予新的活力’。
has given 是现在完成时，说明影响延续到现在。

expressions.text: give a new lease of life to

sentences.sentence: When the Lumière Brothers and other pioneers began showing off this new invention, it was by no means obvious how it would be used.
sentences.translation: 当卢米埃尔兄弟和其他先驱开始展示这项新发明时，人们完全不清楚它将如何被使用。

structure.text: When the Lumière Brothers and other pioneers began showing off this new invention
it was by no means obvious

difficult_points: how it would be used 是主语从句，说明‘什么并不明显’。
would be used 是过去将来时的被动语态，表示当时对未来用途的推测。

sentences.sentence: All that mattered at first was the wonder of movement.
sentences.translation: 起初，唯一重要的是运动带来的奇观。
difficult_points: that mattered at first 是定语从句，修饰 All。
wonder of movement 指影像能够运动这一令人惊奇的现象。

sentences.sentence: Indeed, some said that, once this novelty had worn off, cinema would fade away.
sentences.translation: 事实上，有些人说，一旦这种新奇感消退，电影就会逐渐消失。

structure.text: that, once this novelty had worn off, cinema would fade away
once this novelty had worn off

difficult_points: that 引导 said 的宾语从句，once 从句嵌在其中作时间条件。
had worn off 使用过去完成时，表示新奇感消退发生在电影消失之前。

sentences.sentence: It was no more than a passing gimmick, a fairground attraction.
sentences.translation: 它不过是昙花一现的小把戏，是游乐场的一种吸引物。

structure.text: no more than a passing gimmick

difficult_points: no more than 表示‘只不过是’，带有否定或贬低意味。
a fairground attraction 是同位语，进一步解释 gimmick。

sentences.sentence: Cinema might, for example, have become primarily a documentary form.
sentences.translation: 例如，电影本来可能主要发展成纪录片形式。
difficult_points: might have become 表示对过去可能发展方向的推测。
for example 是插入语，用来引出一种假设。

sentences.sentence: Or it might have developed like television—as a strange, noisy transfer of music, information and narrative.
sentences.translation: 或者，它也可能像电视一样发展成一种奇怪而嘈杂的音乐、信息和叙事传输形式。

structure.text: as a strange, noisy transfer of music, information and narrative

difficult_points: might have developed 与上一句并列，继续表示过去未发生的可能路径。
as a strange... 是补充说明 developed 的结果或形式。

sentences.sentence: But what happened was that it became, overwhelmingly, a medium for telling stories.
sentences.translation: 但实际情况是，电影最终压倒性地成为了讲故事的媒介。

structure.text: that it became, overwhelmingly, a medium for telling stories

difficult_points: what happened 是主语从句，意为‘实际发生的事情’。
for telling stories 是介词 for 加动名词，说明 medium 的用途。

sentences.sentence: Originally these were conceived as short stories—early producers doubted the ability of audiences to concentrate for more than the length of a reel.
sentences.translation: 起初，这些作品被构想成短篇故事——早期制片人怀疑观众能否集中注意力超过一卷胶片的时长。

structure.text: early producers doubted the ability of audiences to concentrate for more than the length of a reel
of audiences to concentrate for more than the length of a reel

difficult_points: were conceived as 是被动结构，表示‘被构想为’。
the ability of audiences to concentrate 中 to concentrate 是 ability 的不定式后置修饰。

sentences.sentence: Then, in 1912, an Italian 2-hour film was hugely successful, and Hollywood settled upon the novel-length narrative that remains the dominant cinematic convention of today.
sentences.translation: 后来，1912年一部意大利的两小时电影大获成功，好莱坞于是确定了小说长度的叙事形式，而这种形式至今仍是电影的主流惯例。

structure.text: an Italian 2-hour film was hugely successful
Hollywood settled upon the novel-length narrative
that remains the dominant cinematic convention of today

difficult_points: and 连接两个并列分句，说明电影成功后好莱坞采取的做法。
that remains... 修饰 the novel-length narrative，说明该叙事形式延续至今。

sentences.sentence: And it has all happened so quickly.
sentences.translation: 而这一切发生得如此迅速。
difficult_points: has happened 使用现在完成时，总结从电影诞生到现在的变化。
all 修饰 happened，概括前文所述的全部发展。

sentences.sentence: Almost unbelievably, it is a mere 100 years since that train arrived and the audience screamed and fled, convinced by the dangerous reality of what they saw, and, perhaps, suddenly aware that the world could never be the same again—that, maybe, it could be better, brighter, more astonishing, more real than reality.
sentences.translation: 几乎难以置信的是，距离那列火车到来、观众因眼前危险的现实而尖叫逃跑，并或许突然意识到世界再也不会相同——也许它会变得更好、更明亮、更惊人、比现实更真实——仅仅过去了100年。

structure.text: it is a mere 100 years since that train arrived and the audience screamed and fled
convinced by the dangerous reality of what they saw
and, perhaps, suddenly aware that the world could never be the same again
that the world could never be the same again
that, maybe, it could be better, brighter, more astonishing, more real than reality

difficult_points: it is a mere 100 years since... 是‘自……以来仅仅过了100年’的时间表达，it 是形式主语。
convinced 和 aware 是过去分词/形容词短语，补充说明观众当时的心理状态。
句末 that 从句具体展开‘世界可能变得更好’这一意识，多个比较级并列。
